seo_description: Current time in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia. Ulaanbaatar Time (ULAT, UTC+8) year-round — no DST since 2017. Live clock and time differences.

faq.question: What time is it in Ulaanbaatar right now?
faq.answer: Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia uses Ulaanbaatar Time (ULAT, UTC+8) year-round. Mongolia abolished Daylight Saving Time in 2017. The IANA identifier is Asia/Ulaanbaatar. Ulaanbaatar shares the UTC+8 offset with Beijing (China), Singapore, Manila (Philippines), and Perth (Australia, in winter). The live clock above shows the current Ulaanbaatar time.

faq.question: What time zone is Ulaanbaatar in?
faq.answer: Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia is in the Ulaanbaatar Time zone (ULAT, UTC+8) year-round. The IANA identifier is Asia/Ulaanbaatar. Mongolia abolished DST in 2017 and now uses UTC+8 permanently. Western Mongolia (provinces like Bayan-Ölgii) uses UTC+7 (Asia/Hovd).

faq.question: Does Ulaanbaatar observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Mongolia abolished Daylight Saving Time in 2017. Prior to 2017, Mongolia observed DST from late March to late September, shifting to UTC+9. Since 2017, Ulaanbaatar and most of Mongolia permanently use ULAT (UTC+8).

faq.question: What is the time difference between Ulaanbaatar and London?
faq.answer: Ulaanbaatar (ULAT, UTC+8) is 8 hours ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During British Summer Time (BST, UTC+1), Ulaanbaatar is 7 hours ahead. Mongolia does not observe DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Ulaanbaatar and New York?
faq.answer: Ulaanbaatar (ULAT, UTC+8) is 13 hours ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US DST (EDT, UTC-4), Ulaanbaatar is 12 hours ahead. Mongolia does not observe DST.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Ulaanbaatar?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Ulaanbaatar are 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM ULAT, Monday through Friday. Ulaanbaatar is Mongolia's capital and home to nearly half the country's population. Mongolia's economy is dominated by mining — copper, gold, and coal exports, particularly to China. The Mongolian Stock Exchange (MSE) is one of the world's smallest by market cap. Ulaanbaatar is the world's coldest capital city.
